front: Performer: shocked the opera world in 1949 by singing Elvira in I Puritani just days after performing Brünnhilde in Die Walküre, replacing an ill singer on short notice
Performer: 'discoverer' and mentor was conductor Tullio Serafin, whom she impressed by sight-reading Act II of Tristan und Isolde
Performer: fired by Met General Manager Rudolf Bing via telegram while rehearsing Medea in Dallas
Performer: only film role was as Medea in Pier Paolo Pasolini's 1969 film

Performer: apocryphally spent one hour perfecting the line 'E avanti a lui tremava tutta Roma' from Tosca; during one performance her wig caught fire and co-star Tito Gobbi leaped on her to put it out
Performer: opened her first recital by singing Isolde's Liebestod in Italian
Performer: subject of Terrence McNally's play Master Class, in which she coaches two sopranos (Sophie and Sharon) near the end of her life
Performer: collaborated with director Luchino Visconti at La Scala in the 1950s on La sonnambula, La vestale, Iphigénie en Tauride, and La traviata
Performer: signature role performed 89 times during the postwar bel canto revival; infamously cancelled the 1958 Rome Opera season opener of this role after Act I, with the President of Italy present
Performer: made her American debut in this role at the 1954 opening of the Lyric Opera of Chicago
Performer: while singing 'Casta Diva,' often used messa di voce — sustaining a single pitch with a crescendo followed by a diminuendo — on the final note
Performer: argued that Act 2, Scene 1 should end after Violetta's aria 'Amami, Alfredo,' cutting out Germont's succeeding aria
Performer: revived the title role of Cherubini's Medea at La Scala's opening week in 1953

Performer: 1955 La Scala production of this Bellini opera, conducted by Bernstein and directed by Visconti, is credited with eliminating excessively flashy soprano antics; Callas headlined the production
Performer: sang the notoriously difficult role of Abigaille in Nabucco in only three performances due to its extreme vocal demands
